--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://image3.slideserve.com/5498471/reactor-measurements-of-sin-2-2-13-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image contains two graphs illustrating reactor neutrino measurement techniques, with the first showing the reactor neutrino spectrum and cross section as functions of energy, and the second depicting the survival probability and 1/r² rate behavior at different baselines, accompanied by explanatory text.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image3.slideserve.com/5754842/methods-to-measure-sin-2-2-13-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image displays methods for measuring reactor neutrino distances, including diagrams of long-distance neutrino beams with detectors, a map of the northern United States highlighting the Soudan mine, and a photo of a reactor in Byron, Illinois with labeled detectors and infrastructure.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://image3.slideserve.com/5498471/key-questions-for-neutrino-osc-phenomenology-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A diagram shows the key questions in neutrino oscillation phenomena, including neutrino mixing angles, CP violation, and the presence of multiple neutrino types, with references to solar, atmospheric, and sterile neutrinos.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://cdn3.slideserve.com/5754842/measurements-of-q-13-using-reactor-neutrinos-n.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image displays a presentation slide titled "Measurements of θ₁₃ Using Reactor Neutrinos," authored by Michael Shaveitz from Columbia University, discussing recent advancements in neutrino oscillation measurements and the importance of reactor experiments for determining the mixing angle θ₁₃.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://images.slideplayer.com/26/8679513/slides/slide_7.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image presents experimental techniques for measuring reactor neutrinos and their distance-dependent effects, including graphs showing flux, spectra, and probability distributions, alongside future reactor experiment setups with detector placement details.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Reactor neutrinos are emitted during nuclear fission. Their detection allows precise distance measurements, leveraging the survival probability of neutrinos over varying baselines.
Accelerator experiments generate neutrinos by colliding protons with targets, producing pions that decay into neutrinos, enabling studies of oscillation phenomena at high energies.

enriched_points.point_title: Key Results from Daya Bay and T2K
enriched_points.expanded_content: Daya Bay measured the mixing angle θ13 with high precision, while T2K provided evidence for neutrino oscillations, both confirming critical parameters in the Standard Model.
Results from these experiments refine our understanding of neutrino mixing parameters, influencing theoretical models and potential implications for CP violation and mass hierarchy.
